title: Eat More Fiber for a Healthy Gut
summary: Eating fiber is very good for your health. Fiber helps your stomach work well and can keep you from getting sick. A new idea called fibermaxxing means eating enough fiber every day. A scientist named Jennifer Lee says this is popular because people want to stay healthy as they get older. She explains that not eating enough fiber can lead to problems like gaining weight or getting diseases like diabetes. Fiber comes from foods like fruits, vegetables, and whole grains. The USDA says adults should eat between 22 and 34 grams of fiber each day, depending on how old they are and if they are a boy or girl. Eating more fiber can make you feel better and help you live a healthier life.

options: It helps your stomach work well

options: You might gain weight or get sick
questions.correct_answer: You might gain weight or get sick

questions.question: How much fiber should adults eat each day according to the USDA?

background_read: Fiber is a type of food found in plants like apples, carrots, and bread. It helps your body digest food and stay healthy. Many people don't eat enough fiber, which can cause health problems. Scientists like Jennifer Lee study how eating more fiber can make people feel better and live longer in good health. The USDA gives guidelines to help people know how much fiber to eat based on their age and gender.
Article_Structure: WHO: Jennifer Lee, a scientist at Tufts University
WHAT: Eating more fiber can improve gut health and prevent diseases
WHERE: In the United States, based on USDA guidelines
WHY: To help people stay healthy as they age and reduce health risks

perspectives.description: Fiber helps digestion and lowers disease risk
